repo_state.head_subject: fix(crew): #742 — depth-cap recursion + briefing scope + cwd fallback (#744)

header.interpretation: Sample is BELOW the issue-#746 sample-size floor (need >=10 distinct projects OR >=50 distinct phases). Locally we observe 2 project directories under wicked-crew/projects, both with NO process-plan.json (likely cleaned up after their crew runs ended). The only non-zero signal is 10 orphan_native entries — all from one historic project ('issue-689-polish-bundle') whose native tasks linger under ~/.claude/tasks/{session_id}/ even though its project directory has been removed. This is the 'tasklist-without-garden-chain' drift class: native tasks outlive the garden chain because TaskList persistence and crew/projects/ cleanup are decoupled. Zero missing_native, zero stale_status, zero phase_drift on this machine — but the absence is a sample-size artifact, not a clean-bill-of-health signal. The cutover staging plan derives its order from PR #735's audit (the substrate/coverage map), not from these counts.
header.sample_adequacy: INSUFFICIENT_FOR_CUTOVER_GATING. Need broader telemetry from at least 10 distinct projects or 50 distinct phases — see staging-plan section 2 for runway analysis.
